data.title: Input Layer and Encoding
data.description: Encode arithmetic expressions and linear equations as directed graphs. Each constant value encoded as a binary tree rooted at its node — no node attributes, identity emerges from structure. Operators and equality as internal nodes. Variable x as anonymous structural node. Input layer immutable. Seed rules provided as bootstrap scaffold — present at generation zero, full GA mutability from generation zero. Seed rule displacement by library-informed rules is the success signal.
data.exit_condition: Expressions reliably converted to base graphs. Sample manually inspected and encoding confirmed sane.

label_format.arithmetic: Terminal value node only — e.g. 7. No wrapper.
label_format.equations: Solved form graph — e.g. x = 3. Irreducible — x = 3 does not collapse to 3.
label_format.collapse_rule: x = x collapses to x. 3 = 3 collapses to 3. x = 3 does not collapse — both sides are distinct.

complexity_ladder: Level 2: One-step solving — e.g. x + 4 = 7, label: x = 3
Level 3: Two-step solving — e.g. 2x + 4 = 10, label: x = 3
Level 4: Variable on both sides — e.g. 2x + 4 = 3x - 1, label: x = 5
